rationale: The task-detail EventSource client now accepts both named log_chunk events and default message events, so live log delivery works regardless of SSE event-name framing.
Live Logs copy now routes through a guarded clipboard helper that skips unsupported environments and swallows rejected writes to avoid unhandled promise rejections.
Stdout/Stderr copy now uses the same guarded clipboard helper, avoiding unhandled clipboard rejections.
Diagnostics copy now uses the guarded clipboard helper so missing clipboard support or rejected writes do not throw.
The task-detail UI tests now save and restore the document.hidden descriptor in afterEach so visibility-state mutations do not leak across tests.
The task-detail UI tests now save and restore navigator.clipboard after each test, preventing clipboard mocks from leaking into later cases.
The Phase 4 plan no longer instructs adding @types/anser and now reflects the implemented no-new-dependencies approach.
The placeholder Phase 4 spec template was replaced with a concrete Live Logs Phase 4 specification covering stories, requirements, edge cases, and success criteria.
This review body is an overview summary rather than a standalone remediation item; the concrete per-file comments were classified individually.
This review body summarizes the two trailing-newline rendering comments, which were handled as the underlying line-specific review comments.
Artifact log parsing now trims a single trailing newline before splitting so the viewer does not render an extra empty row from merged-tail content.
SSE log-chunk parsing now trims a single trailing newline before splitting so live chunks do not add empty rows after each emitted line.
